description: A gallery container displaying a grid of profile cards, each linking to a staff or student profile page.

props.name: profiles
props.type: Array<{ href: string; imageSrc: string; imageAlt: string; quote: string; name: string; subhead: string; subhead2: string }>

stories.name: Default
stories.description: Two profile cards in a gallery

profiles.quote: Your career aspirations won't just be given to you – you have to make important decisions yourself and find out what makes you tick
profiles.name: Daniel Smith

profiles.quote: How my Chemical Engineering degree laid the groundwork for a meaningful career
profiles.name: Thomas

stories.name: ThreeProfiles
stories.description: Three profile cards in a gallery

profiles.imageAlt: Photograph of alumni Thomas Searle longboarding in the peaks
profiles.quote: The course content is really varied - you learn a lot of really interesting things and there's a lot of avenues that you can go down
profiles.name: Thomas Searle
profiles.subhead2: MEng Electrical and Electronic Engineering with a Year in Industry

profiles.imageAlt: Photograph of Aiman outside the General Electric Renewable Energy offices
profiles.quote: I got a job! - Aiman's journey with General Electric Renewable Energy, from a placement year to a graduate job
profiles.name: Ameerul Aiman Bin Shahizam
profiles.subhead2: Electrical and Electronic Engineering, took an Industrial Placement Year

profiles.imageAlt: A photograph taken of MEng Electrical and Electronic Engineering student Wei Theng Haw
profiles.quote: Wei Theng's MEng Electrical and Electronic Engineering experience
profiles.name: Wei Theng Haw

notes:
Each item in the profiles array maps to a ProfileCard (a.profilecard). The ProfileCard component already covers .profilecard, .profilecard .name, .profilecard .subhead, .profilecard .subhead2, .profilecard p, and .profilecard img — the .profile-gallery variants of those selectors are identical and are covered by rendering ProfileCard within this gallery. The quote text appears as a bare

inside the profilecard before the name span.
